data.content: I can see the project — the express delivery test expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m`. Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically on save:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload while iterating on failing tests. Let me start it up for them. The express test is failing (returns 10.00m but expects 15.00m).

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so they can iterate.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-30ced24bc72d48c3bb52bfce9021c87a && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test with hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expects `15.00` but gets `10.00`

The watcher will automatically re-run tests whenever you save a file. To fix the failing test, update the `"express"` case in `CalculateShipping` (line 23) from `10.00m` to `15.00m` — or adjust the test expectation. Just save the file and the watcher will pick it up.
